title: Sector Performance Drivers: Why Mega-Cap Exposure Matters
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Mega-cap companies significantly influence sector returns, but ETFs often underweight them due to regulatory caps, creating a gap in sector exposure that can mi
excerpt: Mega-cap companies significantly influence sector returns, but ETFs often underweight them due to regulatory caps, creating a gap in sector exposure that can mislead investors about their actual holdings. BlackRock's strategies like TOPT help close this gap.

content.introduction: Mega-cap companies significantly influence sector returns, but ETFs often underweight them due to regulatory caps, creating a gap in sector exposure that can mislead investors about their actual holdings. BlackRock's strategies like TOPT help close this gap.
body: *   [Investments](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights/fine-tuning-megacaps-build-etfs). *   [Retirement](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights/fine-tuning-megacaps-build-etfs). *   [Tools](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights/fine-tuning-megacaps-build-etfs). *   [Resources](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights/fine-tuning-megacaps-build-etfs). *   [About Us](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights/fine-tuning-megacaps-build-etfs). *   [Advisors](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights/fine-tuning-megacaps-build-etfs). *   [Our top investment strategies](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/investments/preparing-portfolios). *   [Invest for after-tax returns](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights/tax-center). *   [Investment Directions](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights/inside-the-market/investment-directions). *   [Portfolio manager views](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights#explore-markets-and-portfolio-articles). *   [My Portfolios](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/tools/my-portfolios). *   [Brand & marketing](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/resources/practice-management/advisor-marketing-strategies). For example, [TOPT](https://www.blackrock.com/us/individual/products/339779/ishares-top-20-u-s-stocks-etf) can close the mega-cap gap bringing exposures closer to benchmark, while [XOEF](https://www.blackrock.com/us/individual/products/344551/ishares-s-p-500-ex-s-p-100-etf) can help [reduce mega-cap and AI exposure](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights/dont-abandon-tech). Closing the mega-cap gap with TOPT:**The average asset manager (AM) moderate model is less exposed to mega-cap stocks relative to broad benchmarks like the MSCI ACWI IMI (Fig.3).14 Adding the iShares Top 20 U.S. Stocks ETF ([TOPT](https://www.blackrock.com/us/individual/products/339779/ishares-top-20-us-stocks-etf)) could close an underweight to mega-cap stocks more efficiently by requiring a smaller portfolio allocation than an S&P 500 fund used for the same purpose. The chart illustrates market-cap breakdowns (Mega, Large, Mid & Small, plus TOPT or IVV allocations).](https://www.blackrock.com/blk-one01-c-assets/cache-1777566331000/images/media-bin/web/financial-professionals/topt.svg). ](https://www.blackrock.com/blk-one01-c-assets/cache-1777566331000/images/media-bin/web/financial-professionals/charts/down-exposure-to-mega-caps2.svg). *   [Our top investment strategies](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/investments/preparing-portfolios). *   [Invest for after-tax returns](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights/tax-center). *   [Investment Directions](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights/inside-the-market/investment-directions). *   [Portfolio manager views](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/insights#explore-markets-and-portfolio-articles). *   [My Portfolios](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/tools/my-portfolios). *   [Brand & marketing](https://www.blackrock.com/us/financial-professionals/resources/practice-management/advisor-marketing-strategies).
In today's rapidly evolving equity market landscape, mega-cap stocks are not only influential—but they have become a defining force shaping

# Understanding the Intersection Between Style Exposure, Sector Rotation, and the Business Cycle. * Using holdings-based style analysis, this research shows that the average funds in the Morningstar large-cap growth and large-cap value categories tend to overweight or underweight certain sectors relative to the broad stock market, while SPDR sector ETFs vary greatly in their exposure to growth and value stocks. * After introducing a relatively simple definition of the business cycle, authors proceed to analyze the performance of sectors through the different phases of the cycle and demonstrate that certain groups of sectors tend to consistently perform better in specific phases of the cycle. * Authors demonstrate how a relatively simple investment strategy that actively rotates between sectors—based on changes in the business cycle—would have consistently and significantly outperformed the broad stock market. Using holdings-based style analysis, this paper shows that the average funds in the Morningstar large-cap growth and large-cap value categories tend to overweight or underweight certain sectors relative to the broad stock market, while SPDR sector ETFs vary greatly in their exposure to growth and value stocks.
